title: polemonium caeruleum subsp. occidentale
intro: Polemonium caeruleum subsp. occidentale, also known as Western Jacob's Ladder, is a perennial flowering plant native to western North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Western Jacob's Ladder can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
